cases.band_rationale: Medium: two genuine defects (vague wording in both FRs, no edge cases) in an otherwise structurally complete spec with measurable success criteria. Ceiling 92 requires the two defects to keep costing at least 8 points; floor 75 leaves room to re-tune both up to high severity (2 x 12 = 24 penalty -> 76) without failing the gate. Engine output at labeling time: 88.
cases.note: Vague/subjective wording and no edge cases; otherwise structurally complete. Authored as a regex unit-test case (uses catalog trigger words verbatim); see paraphrased-defects for the non-circular counterpart.

cases.band_rationale: High: a genuinely good spec that deliberately contains trigger-adjacent wording in benign contexts ('simple'/'quick-add'/'faster' in Out of Scope, 'latency' with a threshold, 'scalable' bounded to 250k rows/day). Ceiling 100 so that FIXING the labeled false positive passes the gate; floor 90 tolerates only that one labeled FP (94) — a second unlabeled finding also trips the unlabeled-unexpected gate. Engine output at labeling time: 94.
cases.note: Precision guard (#53b/#56): prose that superficially resembles trigger words but is fine. SPEC-AMBIGUOUS-WORDING fires on 'scalable' in FR-003 even though the requirement is fully quantified (250,000 rows/day, 10-minute window) — a labeled known false positive: it counts in the precision denominator but not the numerator, keeping the metric honest while the gate passes.

cases.band_rationale: Low: a half-authored template (unfilled placeholder title, TODO acceptance, unresolved auth decision) must fail the default 70 gate decisively, so the ceiling is 60; floor 30 asserts the engine still credits the sections that do exist. Engine output at labeling time: 46.
cases.note: Unresolved clarification markers, placeholders, and a TODO instead of acceptance criteria. The extras are genuine secondary defects (no Edge Cases section; 'Login should work well' is unmeasurable), labeled so they count toward precision without being this case's point.

cases.band_rationale: High: zero defects by construction (measurable criteria, edge cases, technology-agnostic, explicit actors). Anything below 95 means a false positive crept into the lint layer.
cases.note: Clean, measurable, technology-agnostic spec. Should produce no findings. Negative control: avoids catalog trigger words; see benign-lookalike for the control that deliberately contains them.

cases.band_rationale: Medium: the spec itself is clean, but the feature has broken traceability (User Story 2 has no task, the Invoice entity is built nowhere) and an unresolved plan decision — real defects diluted by the artifact-weighted mean because spec/data-model score 100. Ceiling 96 requires the three defects to keep costing at least 4 overall points; floor 84 leaves room for severity re-tuning of the traceability findings. Engine output at labeling time: 91.7.
cases.note: Multi-artifact Spec-Kit case (#57): spec + plan + tasks + data-model routed through the same discovery the CLI uses. Exercises the cross-artifact layer (story->task, entity->task) and plan checks (unresolved marker in Technical Context); the plan's Constitution Check passes so PLAN-CONSTITUTION-UNCHECKED must NOT fire.

cases.band_rationale: Medium, deliberately widened DOWNWARD (#55): a spec whose FRs are tech mandates ('build with React and Postgres') deserves roughly 65-80 by external judgment (IBM RQA / QVscribe would weight stack leakage heavily), while the current engine scores it 90. Floor 65 lets the tracked severity re-tuning land without the gate resisting it; ceiling 92 requires the defects to keep costing at least 8 points. Engine output at labeling time: 90.
cases.note: Tech-stack detail and speculative future features in a spec. REQ-COMPOUND is a genuine extra: FR-001 bundles React + Postgres + websockets in one requirement. Calibration gap: current scoring (~90) is lenient versus external judgment; the band floor is set so fixing that leniency PASSES the gate.

cases.band_rationale: Medium-low: every stated quality attribute (latency, availability, scalability) is unverifiable and the success criteria are unmeasurable — the spec gives an implementer no target to hit. Ceiling 84 requires those gaps to keep costing at least 16 points; floor 60 leaves room to re-tune all three findings to high severity (36 penalty -> 64). Engine output at labeling time: 76.
cases.note: NFRs (latency/availability/scalability) with no thresholds; non-measurable success. The ambiguous-wording extra is genuine: 'highly scalable' in FR-002 is exactly the vague phrasing the rule exists for.

cases.band_rationale: Medium: localized defects — one OpenSpec requirement with no scenario, vague wording ('simple') inside a requirement, and a proposal missing its Impact section (the latter costs score via a required-section finding that carries no pitfall id). Ceiling 97 requires the defects to keep costing at least 3 overall points; floor 86 leaves room for severity re-tuning. Engine output at labeling time: 93.6.
cases.note: OpenSpec-layout case (#57): a change directory (proposal + tasks + delta spec) auto-detected by the OpenSpec adapter through the same discovery path the CLI uses. The 'Digest opt-out' requirement has no #### Scenario; the proposal omits '## Impact' (scored, but reported without a pitfall id, so it is asserted via the score band rather than expect_pitfalls).

cases.band_rationale: Anti-circularity case (#53a): the true quality is LOW-medium — five real defects — but the engine currently sees only one, scoring 88. Floor 55 is the external judgment of where the score belongs once the known misses are caught (catching all four adds ~20 penalty -> 68), so closing those gaps PASSES the gate; ceiling 92 requires the one currently-caught defect to keep costing at least 8 points. Engine output at labeling time: 88.
cases.note: Paraphrase case: the same defect classes as ambiguous/nfr-unquantified/impl-leak, worded to avoid the catalog's trigger regexes. Lint SHOULD ideally catch: 'snappy'/'lightweight'/'smoothly' (ambiguous wording, FR-001/FR-002); 'keep working when the whole company checks it at the same moment' (unquantified load NFR, FR-002); 'could later grow a spreadsheet export' (speculative feature, FR-003); 'CockroachDB'/'Svelte' (impl-detail leak, FR-004). Only the digit-based structural check (non-measurable success) generalizes to this phrasing today. These are labeled known_misses: excluded from gated recall, reported in recall_incl_known_misses so the miss stays visible.

cases.band_rationale: Low-medium: a Draft spec with an unresolved decision inside an FR, retained template header ('$ARGUMENTS'), a leaked API style, and no edge cases must fail the default 70 gate, so the ceiling is 70; floor 50 asserts the engine still credits the real user story and measurable success criterion. Engine output at labeling time: 64.
cases.note: Real-world-derived case (#53c): adapts the flawed spec shape seen in real Spec-Kit repos (e.g. the polymarket-kit MCP-server spec pattern) — a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] marker left inside FR-003, the template's '$ARGUMENTS' input line retained, 'REST API' leaked into an FR, and the Review & Acceptance Checklist kept with every box unchecked. Known gap (not a labeled miss because no catalog rule covers it): lint does not flag a retained, fully-unchecked review checklist — SPEC-LEFTOVER-PLACEHOLDER only fires here because of '$ARGUMENTS'.
